title: Logic and Reason
editorialSummary: Logical argument structures and fallacies grouped by category. False trail: Modus Ponens and Hypothetical Syllogism both involve conditional statements (if-then), tempting players to group them with 'Affirming the Consequent' — a fallacy that also uses conditionals — but the valid/invalid distinction is the correct sorting principle. 'Four Terms' is the formal fallacy of using a term in two different senses across premises. All four formal fallacies listed are standard entries in informal logic textbooks.
